candidateEndpoints.description:
Gets all of the users in the system and their information. The result is limited to what the request sender has permission to view.
**Permissons required**: - `view-d-room`: Required to view direct messages - `view-full-other-user-info`: Required to view complete user information (e.g., account creation date, last login) - `view-outside-room`: (Only required if the setting `Apply_permission_view-outside-room` is enabled on under **Settings** > **General** > **Rest API**). Required to view rooms that the user is not a member of. ### Changelog | Version | Description | | ---------------- | ------------| |0.49.0 | `Count` and `offset` query parameters supported. | |0.35.0 | Added |

inputs.name: X-Auth-Token
inputs.description: The authenticated user token.

inputs.name: X-User-Id
inputs.description: The authenticated user ID.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: This parameter allows you to use [MongoDB query](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/operator/query/) operators to search for specific data. For example, to query users with a name that contains the letter "g": query=`{ "name": { "$regex": "g" } }`. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#query-and-fields) to learn more.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: This parameter accepts a JSON object with properties that have a value of 1 or 0 to include or exclude them in the response. For example, to only retrieve the usernames of users: fields=`{ "username": 1 }`. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#query-and-fields) to learn more.

inputs.name: offset
inputs.description: Number of items to "skip" in the query, i.e. requests return count items, skipping the first offset items. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#pagination) to learn more.

inputs.name: count
inputs.description: How many items to return. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#pagination) to learn more.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description:
Sort the users in ascending (`1`) or descending (`-1`) order. The value must be entered as a JSON string. The options are as follows:
 * `status`: Sort by users' status. For example, `sort={"status":1}` (this maps to the `active` status).
 * `createdAt`: Sort by the time of user creation. For example, `sort={"createdAt":-1}`
 * `sort`: Sort by user name. For example, `sort={"name":1}`

candidateEndpoints.description:
Retrieves information about a user. The result is limited only to what you have access to view.

From version `7.0.0`, this endpoint no longer supports the `fields` parameter, even when the `ALLOW_UNSAFE_QUERY_AND_FIELDS_API_PARAMS: true` environment variable is set. Instead, use the `includeUserRooms` parameter.

### Changelog
| Version      | Description |
| ---------------- | ------------|
|7.0.0             | Removed the `fields` query parameter       |
|3.4.0             | Added `unread` property inside `rooms` object       |
|0.70.0            | Added `rooms` property to response if the user request it and has the `view-other-user-channels` permission       |
|0.49.0            | Updated to support `userId` or `username`       |
|0.48.0            | Renamed to `users.info`       |
|0.35.0            | Added       |

candidateEndpoints.description:
Add a user or bulk users to a channel.

For a user to invite other users, they must match at least one of the following premises:
- The user is part of a room of any type and has the `add-user-to-joined-room` permission.
- The user is part of a public room `(t: 'c')` and has the `add-user-to-any-c-room` permission.
- The user is part of a private room `(t: 'p')` and has the `add-user-to-any-p-room` permission.

### Changelog

| Version | Description               |
| ------- | ------------------------- |
| 0.48.0  | Added                  |

candidateEndpoints.description:
Send messages to channels or users on your workspace.

**Note**: You can only send alias and avatar properties if your user has the `message-impersonate permission`. We implemented this rule to avoid users impersonating other users. By default, only the bot role has this permission, but that can be changed in **Manage** -> **Permissions** -> **message-impersonate**. 

### Change Log
| Version | Description |
| ------- |---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 6.8.0   | Allow custom fields in messages                                              | 
| 2.4.1   | Document existing method to post reply/threaded messages                     |
| 2.4.0   | Added validation on user's identity                                          |
| 0.49.0  | The `channel` can now be a room's id.                                        |
| 0.48.0  | Information about the sent message is now returned.                          |
| 0.17.0  | Messages aren't always processed as from BOT and urls are parsed by default. |
| 0.14.0  | Internally using `processWebhookMessage` which enabled more features.        |
| 0.13.0  | Added                                                                        |

candidateEndpoints.description:
The difference between `chat.postMessage` and `chat.sendMessage` is that `chat.sendMessage` allows for passing a value for `_id` and the other one doesn't. Also, `chat.sendMessage` only sends it to one channel whereas the other one allows for sending to more than one channel at a time.
**Note**: You can only send alias and avatar properties if your user has the `message-impersonate permission`. We implemented this rule to avoid users impersonating other users. By default, only the bot role has this permission, but that can be changed in **Manage** -> **Permissions** -> **message-impersonate**. ### Change Log | Version | Description | | ------- | ----------------------------------- | | 6.8.0 | Allow custom fields in messages | | 6.4.0 | Add `previewUrls` param | | 2.4.0 | Added validation on user's identity | | 0.60.0 | Added |

candidateEndpoints.description:
Send messages to channels or users on your workspace.

**Note**: You can only send alias and avatar properties if your user has the `message-impersonate permission`. We implemented this rule to avoid users impersonating other users. By default, only the bot role has this permission, but that can be changed in **Manage** -> **Permissions** -> **message-impersonate**. 

### Change Log
| Version | Description |
| ------- |---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 6.8.0   | Allow custom fields in messages                                              | 
| 2.4.1   | Document existing method to post reply/threaded messages                     |
| 2.4.0   | Added validation on user's identity                                          |
| 0.49.0  | The `channel` can now be a room's id.                                        |
| 0.48.0  | Information about the sent message is now returned.                          |
| 0.17.0  | Messages aren't always processed as from BOT and urls are parsed by default. |
| 0.14.0  | Internally using `processWebhookMessage` which enabled more features.        |
| 0.13.0  | Added                                                                        |

candidateEndpoints.description:
The difference between `chat.postMessage` and `chat.sendMessage` is that `chat.sendMessage` allows for passing a value for `_id` and the other one doesn't. Also, `chat.sendMessage` only sends it to one channel whereas the other one allows for sending to more than one channel at a time.
**Note**: You can only send alias and avatar properties if your user has the `message-impersonate permission`. We implemented this rule to avoid users impersonating other users. By default, only the bot role has this permission, but that can be changed in **Manage** -> **Permissions** -> **message-impersonate**. ### Change Log | Version | Description | | ------- | ----------------------------------- | | 6.8.0 | Allow custom fields in messages | | 6.4.0 | Add `previewUrls` param | | 2.4.0 | Added validation on user's identity | | 0.60.0 | Added |

requestBody.description: **Some important things to note about the `previewUrls` parameter include:** * If the `previewUrls` array is empty, no URL will be previewed. * If the `previewUrls` parameter isn't sent, all URLs (up to a maximum of five external URLs) will be previewed. * If the message contains attachments or quotes, no URL is previewed. * Internal URLs are not considered in the `previewUrls` array. * A maximum of five external URLs is previewed per message. If there are more than 5 external URLs, no URL is previewed. > URLs that include the same text as the **Site URL** are referred to as Internal URLs.

inputs.description:
Sort the users in ascending (`1`) or descending (`-1`) order. The value must be entered as a JSON string. The options are as follows:
 * `status`: Sort by users' status. For example, `sort={"status":1}` (this maps to the `active` status).
 * `createdAt`: Sort by the time of user creation. For example, `sort={"createdAt":-1}`
 * `sort`: Sort by user name. For example, `sort={"name":1}`

candidateEndpoints.description:
Add a user or bulk users to a channel.

For a user to invite other users, they must match at least one of the following premises:
- The user is part of a room of any type and has the `add-user-to-joined-room` permission.
- The user is part of a public room `(t: 'c')` and has the `add-user-to-any-c-room` permission.
- The user is part of a private room `(t: 'p')` and has the `add-user-to-any-p-room` permission.

Action: channel.ensure
Query: onboard a new user(create if doesnt exist) to a channel(create if not exists) with a welcome message
Available input:
{{steps.invite_member.result}}

steps.description: Add the user to the target channel.
steps.purpose: Add the user to the target channel.

candidateEndpoints.description:
Add a user or bulk users to a channel.

For a user to invite other users, they must match at least one of the following premises:
- The user is part of a room of any type and has the `add-user-to-joined-room` permission.
- The user is part of a public room `(t: 'c')` and has the `add-user-to-any-c-room` permission.
- The user is part of a private room `(t: 'p')` and has the `add-user-to-any-p-room` permission.

### Changelog

| Version | Description               |
| ------- | ------------------------- |
| 0.48.0  | Added                  |

inputs.name: X-Auth-Token
inputs.description: The `authToken` of the authenticated user.

inputs.name: X-User-Id
inputs.description: The `userId` of the authenticated user.

inputs.name: roomId
inputs.description: The channel's ID.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.description: The user id to be invited.

inputs.name: userIds
inputs.description: An array of the userId of users to be invited

candidateEndpoints.description:
Send messages to channels or users on your workspace.

**Note**: You can only send alias and avatar properties if your user has the `message-impersonate permission`. We implemented this rule to avoid users impersonating other users. By default, only the bot role has this permission, but that can be changed in **Manage** -> **Permissions** -> **message-impersonate**. 

### Change Log
| Version | Description |
| ------- |---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 6.8.0   | Allow custom fields in messages                                              | 
| 2.4.1   | Document existing method to post reply/threaded messages                     |
| 2.4.0   | Added validation on user's identity                                          |
| 0.49.0  | The `channel` can now be a room's id.                                        |
| 0.48.0  | Information about the sent message is now returned.                          |
| 0.17.0  | Messages aren't always processed as from BOT and urls are parsed by default. |
| 0.14.0  | Internally using `processWebhookMessage` which enabled more features.        |
| 0.13.0  | Added                                                                        |

inputs.name: X-Auth-Token
inputs.description: The `authToken` of the authenticated user.

inputs.name: X-User-Id
inputs.description: The `userId` of the authenticated user.

inputs.name: alias
inputs.description: This will cause the message's name to appear as the given alias, but your username will still be displayed.

inputs.name: avatar
inputs.description: If provided, the avatar will be displayed as the provided image URL.

inputs.name: emoji
inputs.description: If provided, the avatar will be displayed as an emoji.

inputs.name: roomId
inputs.description: The room ID or an array of room IDs where the message is to be sent. You can use channel name or username. The channel name must have the `#` prefix. `@` refers to username.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: The message text to send, it is optional because of attachments.

inputs.name: parseUrls
inputs.description: Set `parseUrls` to `false` to prevent Rocket.Chat from generating link previews when the message in `text` contains a URL.

inputs.name: tmid
inputs.description: The message ID of the original message to reply to or to create a thread on.

inputs.name: customFields
inputs.description: You can add custom fields for messages. For example, set priorities for messages. You must enable this option and define the validation in the workspace settings. See the Message settings for further information.

inputs.name: channel
inputs.description: The channel ID or an array of channel IDs where the message is to be sent. You can use channel name or username. The channel name must have the `#` prefix. `@` refers to username.

candidateEndpoints.description:
Gets all of the users in the system and their information. The result is limited to what the request sender has permission to view.
**Permissons required**: - `view-d-room`: Required to view direct messages - `view-full-other-user-info`: Required to view complete user information (e.g., account creation date, last login) - `view-outside-room`: (Only required if the setting `Apply_permission_view-outside-room` is enabled on under **Settings** > **General** > **Rest API**). Required to view rooms that the user is not a member of. ### Changelog | Version | Description | | ---------------- | ------------| |0.49.0 | `Count` and `offset` query parameters supported. | |0.35.0 | Added |

inputs.name: X-Auth-Token
inputs.description: The authenticated user token.

inputs.name: X-User-Id
inputs.description: The authenticated user ID.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: This parameter allows you to use [MongoDB query](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/operator/query/) operators to search for specific data. For example, to query users with a name that contains the letter "g": query=`{ "name": { "$regex": "g" } }`. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#query-and-fields) to learn more.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: This parameter accepts a JSON object with properties that have a value of 1 or 0 to include or exclude them in the response. For example, to only retrieve the usernames of users: fields=`{ "username": 1 }`. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#query-and-fields) to learn more.

inputs.name: offset
inputs.description: Number of items to "skip" in the query, i.e. requests return count items, skipping the first offset items. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#pagination) to learn more.

inputs.name: count
inputs.description: How many items to return. Refer to the [official documentation](https://developer.rocket.chat/apidocs/query-parameters#pagination) to learn more.

inputs.name: sort
inputs.description:
Sort the users in ascending (`1`) or descending (`-1`) order. The value must be entered as a JSON string. The options are as follows:
 * `status`: Sort by users' status. For example, `sort={"status":1}` (this maps to the `active` status).
 * `createdAt`: Sort by the time of user creation. For example, `sort={"createdAt":-1}`
 * `sort`: Sort by user name. For example, `sort={"name":1}`
